Politics.headline: Eric Swalwell Exits California Governor Race After Sex Assault Allegations
Politics.detail: US Representative Eric Swalwell has ended his campaign for California governor following allegations of sexual assault.

Politics.headline: Trump News at a Glance: President Renews Threat to Iranian Power Plants and Bridges After Talks Fail
Politics.detail: Following the failure of peace talks, President Trump has reiterated his threat to target Iranian power plants and bridges.

Politics.headline: Oil Price Tops $100 a Barrel After Peace Talks Fail and Trump Orders Blockade
Politics.detail: The price of oil has surged above $100 a barrel after peace talks collapsed and President Trump ordered a blockade.

Politics.headline: Trump Unleashes Tirade Against "Weak," "Terrible" Pope Leo
Politics.detail: President Trump has launched a tirade against Pope Leo, calling him "weak" and "terrible."

Politics.headline: Voters head to polls in 3 federal byelections that could deliver majority Liberal government
Politics.detail: Voters in two Ontario ridings and one in Quebec will head to the polls on Monday for a slate of crucial federal byelections that could deliver the Liberals a majority government.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil prices surge past $100 after Trump orders Hormuz blockade
Finance & Economy.detail: Following failed peace talks, oil prices have climbed above $100 a barrel after former President Trump ordered a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, reigniting fears of a global energy crisis.

Finance & Economy.headline: S&P Global Market Intelligence's 2026 Private Equity Survey Shows Fundraising Confidence Rising as Managers Pivot to Operational Value
Finance & Economy.detail: A new report reveals that private equity fund managers are focusing on value creation through operational improvements amidst economic uncertainty, with most expecting deal volumes to hold steady or improve in 2026.

Finance & Economy.headline: Baker Hughes Announces Sale of Waygate Technologies to Hexagon
Finance & Economy.detail: Baker Hughes has agreed to sell its Waygate Technologies business to Hexagon for approximately $1.45 billion in cash, as part of its strategic approach to value creation and portfolio management.

Finance & Economy.headline: Volkswagen Group maintains stable market share in declining global market in Q1
Finance & Economy.detail: The Volkswagen Group reported a 4% decrease in global deliveries for Q1 2026, but maintained its market share despite challenging economic and geopolitical conditions and a declining worldwide automotive market.

Finance & Economy.headline: Türkiye's Şimşek joins global finance chiefs' talks amid Mideast shock
Finance & Economy.detail: Treasury and Finance Minister Mehmet Şimşek is in the U.S. for high-level meetings with global investors and financial institutions during the IMF-World Bank spring meetings, amidst concerns over the economic impact of the war in the Middle East.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Plans to Finish Outlook Lite Shut Down Next Month
Technology.detail: The Outlook Lite app will stop working on May 25, following its October delisting from the Android store.

Technology.headline: Sutter Health and MemorialCare Hit With Class Action Over AI Transcription
Technology.detail: AI transcription technology was allegedly used to capture and transcribe physician–patient conversations, using microphone-enabled devices in examination rooms, without necessary consent.

Technology.headline: China wants AI to prepare school lessons and mark homework
Technology.detail: China is exploring the use of AI to assist in preparing school lessons and grading homework, among other applications.

Technology.headline: Police arrest a suspect in a Molotov attack at OpenAI CEO's San Francisco home
Technology.detail: A suspect has been arrested in connection with a Molotov cocktail attack that occurred at the San Francisco home of OpenAI's CEO.

Technology.headline: AI-Powered Cyberattacks Raise Alarm Among IT Leaders
Technology.detail: Nearly eight out of 10 global IT decision-makers say artificial intelligence poses a significant security threat as AI-driven attacks accelerate.

Science.headline: Colossal 'Cosmic Volcano' Erupts in Deep Space
Science.detail: A supermassive black hole in galaxy J1007+3540 roars back to life after nearly 100 million years of silence, with astronomers capturing stunning radio images of fresh jets blasting outward.

Science.headline: Lifespan Is More Genetic Than Previously Thought
Science.detail: A new study from the Weizmann Institute indicates that genetics play a more significant role in determining lifespan than previously believed, challenging the notion that environment and chance are the primary factors.

Science.headline: New Way to Detect Gravitational Waves Proposed
Science.detail: Scientists have proposed a novel method for detecting gravitational waves by observing how they alter the light emitted by atoms.

Science.headline: Giant New Moon Scar Is a Once-in-a-Century Crater, Scientists Discover
Science.detail: A rare impact event on the moon has created a massive new crater, offering scientists a unique opportunity to study the lunar surface and impact processes.

Science.headline: Twice-Yearly Injection May Change High Blood Pressure Treatment
Science.detail: A new approach to treating high blood pressure involves a twice-yearly injection, potentially revolutionizing how this common condition is managed.

Health.headline: WHO warns Gaza aid restrictions hinder health response, raise disease risk
Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) has warned that ongoing restrictions on the entry of medical aid into Gaza are severely hindering the health response and increasing the risk of disease outbreaks, with life-saving medical supplies stalled and fuel shortages threatening the collapse of health services. The situation has moved beyond the immediate impact of attacks, with restricted access to healthcare services now placing Palestinians at growing risk.

Health.headline: Philippine President Marcos debunks health rumors with jumping jacks
Health.detail: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. performed jumping jacks and jogged briefly in front of journalists to disprove rumors of his failing health, stating that those claiming he is sick or paralyzed are liars.  Marcos addressed the rumors after a brief absence from public view in January due to an abdominal ailment, challenging anyone who doubts his health to exercise with him.

Health.headline: NHS App £160 million contract awarded to IBM
Health.detail: The NHS App, which has reached over 39 million users, awarded a £160 million contract to IBM to develop secure, reliable, and user-centered pathways and services at scale, with the aim of giving people much more ownership of their healthcare. The app aims to become a “doctor in their pockets” for patients, offering access, empowerment, and care planning, and creating a “full front door to the entire NHS”.

Health.headline: UConn Health Offers New Level of Precision in Cancer Care
Health.detail: UConn Health's Carole and Ray Neag Comprehensive Cancer Center is now offering advanced radiotherapy with the TrueBeam Linear Accelerator, which provides unprecedented levels of safety and accuracy. The technology delivers pinpoint doses of radiation to the target tissue, minimizing exposure to surrounding tissue and promising improved clinical outcomes for patients with various cancers.

Health.headline: European Doctors Call Gambling Major Public Health Problem
Health.detail: European doctors are calling gambling a major public health problem. No summary available.

Sports.headline: RCB Star Punished For Disobeying Umpires During MI Match, Pandya Fined
Sports.detail: A Royal Challengers Bangalore (RCB) player was penalized for not following umpire instructions during a match against Mumbai Indians (MI), and Hardik Pandya also received a fine.

Sports.headline: Record Prize Fund Of Rs 82 Crore Announced For Women's T20 World Cup 2026
Sports.detail: The Women's T20 World Cup 2026 will feature a record prize fund of Rs 82 Crore.

Sports.headline: Man City closes in on Arsenal in title race, beats Chelsea
Sports.detail: Manchester City defeated Chelsea, tightening the gap between them and Arsenal in the title race.

Sports.headline: NBA: Doc Rivers isn't expected back as Bucks' coach next season
Sports.detail: Doc Rivers is not anticipated to return as the coach for the Milwaukee Bucks in the upcoming season.

Sports.headline: McIlroy joins elite club with back-to-back Masters wins
Sports.detail: Rory McIlroy has won back-to-back Masters titles, putting him in an elite class of golfers.

Entertainment.headline: Britney Spears Checks Into Rehab After DUI Arrest
Entertainment.detail: Following her arrest for driving under the influence in March, Britney Spears has voluntarily checked into a rehabilitation facility to address substance abuse issues.

Entertainment.headline: New documentary reveals boyband 98 Degrees had age-of-consent manual while touring in 90s
Entertainment.detail: A new documentary has revealed that the boyband 98 Degrees had an age-of-consent manual while they were touring in the 1990s.

Entertainment.headline: Coachella 2026: Katy Perry REACTS to Justin Bieber's YouTube-Themed Set.
Entertainment.detail: Katy Perry was seen reacting to Justin Bieber's YouTube-themed set at Coachella 2026.

Entertainment.headline: Asha Bhosle, legendary playback singer, passes away at 92 in Mumbai
Entertainment.detail: Asha Bhosle, a legendary playback singer, has passed away in Mumbai at the age of 92.

Entertainment.headline: No Doubt's Tom Dumont Diagnosed With Early Onset Parkinson's
Entertainment.detail: Tom Dumont, from the band No Doubt, has been diagnosed with early onset Parkinson's disease, but he still plans to perform at Sphere this May.

Crime & Justice.headline: You Hug Your Kids Extra Hard Brooklyn Da Reflects On Personal Connection To Fatal Shooting Of 7 Month Old Baby In Exclusive Interview
Crime & Justice.detail: Brooklyn District Attorney Eric Gonzalez described his emotional visit to the family of a 7-month-old baby killed by a stray bullet, emphasizing the devastating impact of the incident and the need for continued efforts to prevent violent crime.

Crime & Justice.headline: New justice puts Constitutional Court in spotlight amid TNI Law petitions
Crime & Justice.detail: A new justice on the Constitutional Court is drawing attention amid petitions related to the TNI Law, which stipulates that troops suspected of general crimes should be tried in civilian courts, a point often criticized due to lighter sentences in military tribunals.

Crime & Justice.headline: Bengal SIR Hearing Live Updates: Supreme Court says 34 lakh excluded from electoral rolls can't vote in 2026 polls
Crime & Justice.detail: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that individuals excluded from electoral rolls in West Bengal due to pending appeals will not be allowed to vote in the upcoming 2026 elections.

Crime & Justice.headline: Machete-wielding man killed by police in New York's Grand Central station
Crime & Justice.detail: A man wielding a machete was fatally shot by police in New York's Grand Central station after he wounded three elderly individuals; authorities are investigating the incident.

Crime & Justice.headline: Police say 1 person killed and 6 injured in shooting at a Chick-fil-A in New Jersey
Crime & Justice.detail: One person was killed and six others were injured in a mass shooting at a Chick-fil-A restaurant in Union, New Jersey, according to police reports.

Education.headline: NATO and Azerbaijan strengthen cooperation on the modernisation of defence education
Education.detail: NATO's Defence Education Enhancement Programme (DEEP) will continue supporting faculty and curriculum development in Azerbaijan, aligning English language training with NATO standards, as requested by Azerbaijan's Ministry of Defence. The cooperation aims to build capabilities to manage an eLearning platform for the entire Azerbaijani military education system.

Education.headline: IIT Kharagpur launches 4 online executive courses in AI, machine learning and tech leadership
Education.detail: IIT Kharagpur has launched four new online executive courses focusing on artificial intelligence, machine learning, and technology leadership, catering to the growing demand for skilled professionals in these fields. These courses aim to equip professionals with the knowledge and skills necessary to excel in the rapidly evolving tech industry.

Education.headline: Northeastern's President Joseph E. Aoun Discusses Academic Achievement and the Future of the University
Education.detail: Northeastern University President Joseph Aoun discussed the university's achievements and future, emphasizing the importance of experiential learning and the integration of humanics, including tech, data, and human literacy, to address the challenges and opportunities presented by AI. He also highlighted the university's strong co-op program and its role in preparing students for a shifting job market.

Education.headline: KCET Exam 2026: KEA warns on fake websites trying to dupe students
Education.detail: The Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA) has issued a warning regarding fake websites attempting to deceive students applying for the KCET 2026 exam. Students are advised to only use the official KEA website for information and application processes.

Education.headline: WVU president selects leading mining engineering educator as his first Presidential Honorary Degree recipient
Education.detail: West Virginia University's president has chosen a distinguished mining engineering educator as the recipient of his first Presidential Honorary Degree. Peng's work has emphasized both theory and practice, validating theory with mining practice and basing mining practice on sound theory.

Lifestyle.headline: Alia Bhatt's trainer shares a 10-minute workout for busy days
Lifestyle.detail: Celebrity trainer Karan Sawhney shares a simple 10-minute workout you can do anywhere, demonstrating how short, consistent routines can improve fitness without equipment or long sessions.

Lifestyle.headline: Grand Slam style: BOSS named Official Lifestyle Outfitter of the Australian Open
Lifestyle.detail: The Australian Open welcomes BOSS as the Official Lifestyle Outfitter from 2027, bringing its blend of tailoring and sportswear to Melbourne Park, dressing staff and elevating fan experiences. The partnership extends beyond the courts with merchandise, retail, and hospitality, aiming to enhance the tournament's atmosphere.

Lifestyle.headline: Living well, living consciously: A thoughtful afternoon at Southlinks Estate
Lifestyle.detail: Southlinks Estate hosted “Living Well, Living Consciously,” a panel discussion on wellness and intentional living, featuring Cristalle Belo-Pitt and Iza Calzado, who emphasized slowing down, prioritizing health, and being present. The discussion highlighted how living in the South supports a balanced lifestyle.

Lifestyle.headline: Meet Jon, the Stray Dog Who's Been 'Working' With Police & Manning Traffic in KL for the Past Two Years
Lifestyle.detail: A stray dog named Jon has been helping traffic police manage traffic along Jalan Parlimen in Kuala Lumpur since 2024, arriving early and 'working' alongside them every morning. Videos of Jon have gone viral, drawing attention from motorists who sometimes give him food.

Lifestyle.headline: This blue, curvy Baldwin Hills house is Black postmodernism in motion
Lifestyle.detail: A unique, blue-hued house in Baldwin Hills, Los Angeles, is examined through the lens of Black postmodernism, which embraces playful and whimsical design elements. The owner discusses the home's architecture and its significance as a space chosen by her family.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The U.S. needs energy from diverse sources to endure supply shocks.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: In a guest opinion piece, Josh Freed argues that the United States requires a diversified energy portfolio to withstand disruptions in supply.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: What Kevin Warsh misunderstands about the Maestro's method.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Adam M. Guren critiques Kevin Warsh's understanding of the methods employed by the 'Maestro'.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: It's high time for this (self-employed) worker revolution.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Jonathan Wolfson argues that it is time for a revolution for self-employed workers.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL — Back to online learning
Opinion/Editorial.detail: To conserve fuel and energy, the Commission on Higher Education has allowed colleges and universities to shift to 100 percent online classes for the remainder of school year 2025-2026.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Disgraced Tony Gonzales should resign.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The San Antonio Express-News editorial board argues that Tony Gonzales should resign and that San Antonio deserves better representation in Congress.

World.headline: Trump says US will blockade strait of Hormuz after Iran peace talks fail
World.detail: Following unsuccessful peace talks, the US President announced a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions in the region. The US military is prepared to impose a naval blockade on all Iranian ports starting Monday.

World.headline: Pope Leo says he does not fear Trump after attack over Iran peace appeal
World.detail: Pope Leo responded to President Trump's criticism of his efforts to broker peace, stating he does not fear the Trump administration. The Pope, who has been critical of the US-Israeli war on Iran, stated his comments were not meant as attacks on anyone.

World.headline: Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power
World.detail: In a significant political shift, the Hungarian opposition, led by Peter Magyar, has defeated Viktor Orbán, ending his 16-year rule. The EU is celebrating the defeat of Hungary's Orban, a Trump ally who defied Brussels.

World.headline: At least six killed in Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon
World.detail: Tragically, at least six people have been killed in recent Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon. This comes as Anthony Albanese urges Israel to stop Lebanon attacks that intensified during Middle East ceasefire.

World.headline: Tear gas fired at India workers demanding higher wages as living costs rise
World.detail: Police used tear gas against protesting workers in India who are demanding higher wages amidst rising living costs. The incident highlights the economic pressures faced by many workers in India.

Zimbabwe.headline: Civil Servants' New Pay Structure Ranges From US$370 To US$900
Zimbabwe.detail: The new pay structure for civil servants in Zimbabwe sees salaries ranging from US$370 to US$900, impacting various government employees.

Zimbabwe.headline: Police Officers Attacked In Brazen Midnight Robbery, Weapons And Vehicle Seized
Zimbabwe.detail: A group of police officers were attacked in a midnight robbery, resulting in the loss of weapons and a vehicle.

Zimbabwe.headline: Bulawayo Town Clerk Christopher Dube's Contract Extended To 2030
Zimbabwe.detail: Christopher Dube, the Bulawayo Town Clerk, has had his contract extended to 2030, ensuring his continued service in the city's administration.

Zimbabwe.headline: Solar-powered labs transform education in Mat South
Zimbabwe.detail: Solar-powered laboratories are revolutionizing education in the Matabeleland South province, providing students with access to modern technology.

Zimbabwe.headline: Botha appointed new Tanganda CEO as Innscor takes charge
Zimbabwe.detail: Botha has been appointed as the new CEO of Tanganda as Innscor Africa Limited takes over management.
